title: Middleware Cors
description: Allow browser-based Studio (`specd-studio-web`, desktop remote) to call an API on a different origin than the static UI host.
context: Allow browser-based Studio (`specd-studio-web`, desktop remote) to call an API on a different origin than the static UI host.

rules: The server MUST accept a configuration list of allowed origins. Preflight `OPTIONS` and actual responses MUST include appropriate `Access-Control-Allow-*` headers for listed origins.
When the Studio client sends credentialed requests (cookies or `Authorization`), CORS configuration MUST set `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials` consistently with the allowed origins list.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.name: Wildcard disabled when credentials enabled
then: AND startup fails or config validation errors

scenarios.name: Omitted origin config uses safe default
when: specd serve runs without cors origins in yaml
then: only same-origin or documented default applies
AND behavior is stable across restarts

scenarios.name: Allow-Credentials set when enabled
then: AND cookies may be sent cross-origin

scenarios.name: Credentials false omits allow-credentials
then: AND anonymous cross-origin reads still work

scenarios.name: Browser blocked request surfaces clear failure
when: UI calls API from disallowed origin with credentials
then: AND Studio connect panel shows CORS hint
